question: ACDEFG is a regular hexagon with center B and circumradius 9. Let I be the perpendicular bisector of segment EB. Point U is the reflection of point G across line I. Let Q be the perpendicular bisector of segment CU. Let P be the reflection of line Q across line I. Point Y is the reflection of point C across line P. Point O is obtained by rotating point Y by angle 5pi/12 about point B. Find the length of segment AO. Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

Let ABCDE be a regular 5-gon with center K and circumradius 2. Let S be the perpendicular bisector of segment KE. Line R is the perpendicular bisector of segment DE. Let J be the reflection of line R across line S. Point Q is the reflection of point D across line J. What is the distance between points Q and B? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

Let ABDEFGHIJ be a regular 9-gon with center C and circumradius 2. Let X be the line through points G and A. Point V is the intersection of line X and segment HB. Point Z is the reflection of point V across point B. Point M is the reflection of point J across segment HB. Find the length of segment MZ. Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.
